title: Bhaal
summary: God of murder and violent death. A feared and reviled deity whose followers carry out ritualistic killings in his name.
content:
## Bhaal

**Title:** The Lord of Murder
**Alignment:** Neutral Evil
**Domains:** Death
**Symbol:** Skull surrounded by a ring of blood droplets
**Pantheon:** Forgotten Realms

Bhaal is the god of murder, assassination, and violent death. Once a mortal adventurer alongside Bane and Myrkul, he ascended to godhood by slaying the previous deity of the dead. Bhaal revels in the act of killing itself, caring nothing for the reasons behind a murder -- only that blood is shed in his name. His dogma holds that every murder committed strengthens him.

The cult of Bhaal operates in secret cells, with followers known as Deathstalkers carrying out ritual murders to honor the Lord of Murder. His clergy maintain hidden shrines in urban underworlds and wild places alike. The Bhaalspawn -- mortal offspring sired by Bhaal before his death during the Time of Troubles -- became the center of a crisis that shook the Sword Coast, as the god sought to use their divine essence to fuel his resurrection.

**Allies and Enemies:** Bhaal was once part of the Dead Three alongside Bane and Myrkul, and these dark gods maintain an uneasy alliance. He is opposed by Helm, Ilmater, Lathander, and virtually every good-aligned deity. Cyric slew Bhaal during the Time of Troubles and claimed his portfolio, though Bhaal has since returned.

metadata_json.symbol: Skull surrounded by a ring of blood droplets

title: Deneir
summary: God of writing, glyphs, and literature. A scholarly deity who encourages the preservation of knowledge through the written word.
content:
## Deneir

**Title:** The Lord of All Glyphs and Images, the Scribe of Oghma
**Alignment:** Neutral Good
**Domains:** Arcana, Knowledge
**Symbol:** Lit candle above an open eye
**Pantheon:** Forgotten Realms

Deneir is the god of writing, cartography, glyphs, images, and literature. He serves as the scribe of Oghma, recording and cataloguing knowledge in all its written forms. Deneir believes that the written word is the most powerful tool for preserving and transmitting knowledge across generations, and his followers dedicate themselves to literacy, calligraphy, and the creation of books and scrolls.

His clergy, known as Glyphscribes, are tireless scribes, librarians, and cartographers who maintain extensive archives and copy manuscripts to ensure their survival. Temples of Deneir serve as libraries and scriptoriums where anyone may come to read or learn to write. His priests invent new alphabets, decipher ancient texts, and create magical glyphs and sigils.

**Allies and Enemies:** Deneir serves Oghma faithfully and is closely allied with Milil and Lliira in the service of knowledge and creative expression. He opposes Cyric, whose portfolio of lies threatens the integrity of written records, and Mask, whose deceptions corrupt the truth.

metadata_json.symbol: Lit candle above an open eye

title: Eldath
summary: Goddess of quiet places, springs, and peace. A gentle deity who offers sanctuary and serenity to the weary.
content:
## Eldath

**Title:** The Quiet One, Goddess of Singing Waters
**Alignment:** Neutral Good
**Domains:** Life, Nature
**Symbol:** Waterfall plunging into a still pool
**Pantheon:** Forgotten Realms

Eldath is the goddess of peace, quiet places, springs, pools, and druid groves. She is one of the most gentle and pacifistic deities in the Realms, embodying the serenity found in a still forest pool or a quiet woodland glade. Eldath's followers believe that true strength lies in peace, compassion, and the rejection of violence.

Her clergy, known as Peacekeepers or Green Walkers, tend sacred pools and groves that serve as sanctuaries where no violence is permitted. These sites are places of healing and meditation, open to all who come in peace. Eldath's priests rarely adventure, but when they do, they serve as mediators and healers, refusing to take up arms except in the most dire circumstances to protect the innocent.

**Allies and Enemies:** Eldath is closely allied with Mielikki, Silvanus, and Chauntea as fellow nature deities. She is the eternal opponent of Tempus, the god of war, though their conflict is philosophical rather than violent. She also opposes Malar and Talos, whose destructive natures threaten the peace she cherishes.

metadata_json.symbol: Waterfall plunging into a still pool

title: Gond
summary: God of craft, smithwork, and invention. A pragmatic deity who inspires innovation and the creation of new devices.
content:
## Gond

**Title:** Wonderbringer, Lord of All Smiths
**Alignment:** True Neutral
**Domains:** Knowledge
**Symbol:** Toothed cog with four spokes
**Pantheon:** Forgotten Realms

Gond is the god of craft, smithing, and invention. He is a restless and creative deity who encourages innovation, experimentation, and the advancement of technology. Gond cares nothing for the moral implications of inventions -- only that they are created. A new siege engine and a new plow are equally worthy in his eyes, as both represent the triumph of ingenuity.

His clergy, known as the Inspired, are smiths, engineers, tinkers, and inventors who maintain workshops and forges as their temples. The Church of Gond sponsors crafting competitions, funds inventors, and maintains repositories of blueprints and schematics. The island nation of Lantan was once the center of Gondar worship, where smokepowder and other advanced technologies were developed under his guidance.

**Allies and Enemies:** Gond serves Oghma in the broader pursuit of knowledge, though his focus on practical application sometimes puts them at odds. He is allied with Lathander, who shares his optimism for progress. He opposes Talos, whose destructive storms ruin the works of craftspeople.

title: Leira
summary: Goddess of illusion and deception. A mysterious deity who revels in misdirection and the blurring of truth and falsehood.
content:
## Leira

**Title:** The Lady of the Mists, the Lady of Deception
**Alignment:** Chaotic Neutral
**Domains:** Trickery
**Symbol:** Point-down triangle containing a swirl of mist
**Pantheon:** Forgotten Realms

Leira is the goddess of illusion, deception, and the concealment of truth behind layers of falsehood. She teaches that nothing is as it seems and that reality is merely a construct that can be bent and reshaped by those with sufficient will and cunning. Leira does not promote malicious lies so much as the philosophical notion that truth is subjective and perception is reality.

Her clergy are secretive and enigmatic, often disguising their true nature even from one another. Temples of Leira are hidden behind illusions or in places where the boundary between real and unreal grows thin. Her followers include illusionists, spies, con artists, and philosophers who question the nature of reality. Worship of Leira was thought to have ended when Cyric seemingly killed her during the Time of Troubles, but her death may itself have been the greatest illusion of all.

**Allies and Enemies:** Leira has few true allies, as her nature makes trust difficult. She opposes Cyric, who claimed to have slain her, and has a complicated relationship with Mask, fellow deity of shadows and deception. She is at odds with Oghma and Deneir, whose pursuit of clear truth contradicts her doctrine.

metadata_json.symbol: Point-down triangle containing a swirl of mist

title: Lliira
summary: Goddess of joy, happiness, and dance. A carefree deity who encourages celebration and the free expression of happiness.
content:
## Lliira

**Title:** Our Lady of Joy, the Joybringer
**Alignment:** Chaotic Good
**Domains:** Life
**Symbol:** Triangle of three six-pointed stars
**Pantheon:** Forgotten Realms

Lliira is the goddess of joy, happiness, dance, festivals, and freedom. She teaches that happiness is a sacred gift meant to be shared freely and that the expression of joy through dance, song, and celebration is the highest form of worship. Lliira abhors suffering and cruelty, believing that every being deserves moments of happiness and that even the darkest times can be brightened by shared joy.

Her clergy, known as Joybringers, are dancers, entertainers, and festival organizers who travel the Realms spreading happiness wherever they go. They organize grand celebrations, tend to the emotionally wounded, and work to lift the spirits of communities in despair. Temples of Lliira are colorful, welcoming places filled with music and laughter, open to all who seek a moment of happiness.

**Allies and Enemies:** Lliira is closely allied with Sune, sharing a love of beauty and pleasure, and with Milil, who provides the music for her dances. She serves as a friend to Deneir and Oghma. She opposes Loviatar, whose cruelty and infliction of pain directly contradicts everything Lliira stands for, and Bane, whose tyranny crushes the freedom she cherishes.

title: Loviatar
summary: Goddess of pain and suffering. A cruel deity who teaches that pain is the path to strength and that suffering purifies the soul.
content:
## Loviatar

**Title:** The Maiden of Pain, the Willing Whip
**Alignment:** Lawful Evil
**Domains:** Death
**Symbol:** Nine-tailed barbed scourge
**Pantheon:** Forgotten Realms

Loviatar is the goddess of pain, agony, suffering, and torment. She teaches that pain is the most powerful force in the world -- more reliable than love, stronger than hope, and more honest than pleasure. Her followers believe that through the giving and receiving of pain, one achieves spiritual purity and strength. Loviatar herself is said to take exquisite pleasure in the suffering of others.

Her clergy, known as Loviatans or Pains, are feared throughout the Realms as torturers and sadists who practice ritual flagellation and painful rites of initiation. Temples of Loviatar are often dungeon-like complexes equipped with instruments of torment. Despite their fearsome reputation, Loviatans also serve as skilled interrogators and are sometimes sought out by those who believe that suffering will atone for their sins.

**Allies and Enemies:** Loviatar is a loyal servant of Bane and is allied with Bhaal, Malar, and Talona in the spread of misery. She has a deep hatred for Ilmater, the god of suffering and martyrdom, whose willingness to absorb the pain of others directly undermines her doctrine. She also opposes Lliira and Sune, whose celebrations of joy mock her ideals.

title: Malar
summary: God of the hunt and bloodlust. A savage deity who embodies the predatory nature of the wild and the thrill of the chase.
content:
## Malar

**Title:** The Beastlord, the Black-Blooded Pard
**Alignment:** Chaotic Evil
**Domains:** Nature
**Symbol:** Clawed paw
**Pantheon:** Forgotten Realms

Malar is the god of the hunt, stalking, bloodlust, and savage beasts. He embodies the brutal, predatory aspect of nature -- the claw and fang, the chase and the kill. Malar teaches that the strong hunt and devour the weak, and that the thrill of the hunt is the purest expression of life. He disdains civilization and all its trappings, viewing cities as cages that trap the predatory spirit.

His clergy, known as the Lords of the Hunt or the People of the Black Blood, conduct ritual hunts called the High Hunts where captives or dangerous beasts are released and pursued through the wilderness. Many of his followers are lycanthropes who embrace their bestial nature as a blessing from the Beastlord. Temples of Malar are crude altars deep in the wilderness, often marked with bloody trophies of past hunts.

**Allies and Enemies:** Malar is loosely allied with Auril, Talos, and Umberlee as part of the Gods of Fury, united in their love of nature's destructive power. He is the sworn enemy of Nobanion, Lurue, and Gwaeron Windstrom. He also opposes Silvanus, Mielikki, and Eldath, who represent the nurturing side of nature.

title: Milil
summary: God of song, poetry, and eloquence. A charismatic deity who inspires bards and all who create beauty through words and music.
content:
## Milil

**Title:** The Lord of Song, the Lord of All Songs
**Alignment:** Neutral Good
**Domains:** Light
**Symbol:** Five-stringed harp made of leaves
**Pantheon:** Forgotten Realms

Milil is the god of song, poetry, eloquence, and creative inspiration. He is the ultimate patron of bards, minstrels, troubadours, and all who weave beauty through words and music. Milil believes that song and poetry are the highest forms of artistic expression, capable of moving hearts, preserving history, and touching the divine. He himself is said to be the most gifted musician among the gods.

His clergy, known as Sorlyn, are musicians, poets, and performers who believe that their art is a form of worship. Temples of Milil resemble concert halls and conservatories where music is taught, compositions are preserved, and performances are held. His priests travel the Realms as wandering minstrels, spreading beauty and culture to even the most remote communities and recording the great deeds of heroes in song.

**Allies and Enemies:** Milil serves Oghma and works closely with Deneir and Lliira in the preservation and celebration of knowledge and art. He is a close friend of Sune, appreciating beauty in all its forms. He opposes Cyric, whose lies corrupt the truth that art seeks to capture, and Bane, whose tyranny silences free expression.

title: Myrkul
summary: God of death, decay, and the dead. A grim deity who once ruled the realm of the dead with an iron hand and hollow heart.
content:
## Myrkul

**Title:** The Lord of Bones, Old Lord Skull
**Alignment:** Neutral Evil
**Domains:** Death
**Symbol:** White human skull on black background
**Pantheon:** Forgotten Realms

Myrkul is the god of death, decay, old age, exhaustion, dusk, and autumn. Once a mortal adventurer who ascended alongside Bane and Bhaal, Myrkul ruled the City of the Dead in the Gray Waste, collecting the souls of the departed with cold efficiency. Unlike Bhaal, who delights in the act of killing, Myrkul is concerned with death as an inevitable end and the fate of the soul thereafter.

Myrkul's clergy are necromancers, embalmers, and gravekeepers who tend to the dead and remind the living that death comes for all. His temples are mausoleums and crypts where the dead are interred with proper rites to prevent their restless rising. Myrkul was slain during the Time of Troubles, his essence bound into the artifact known as the Crown of Horns, but he has since returned to claim his place among the gods once more.

**Allies and Enemies:** Myrkul was part of the Dead Three alongside Bane and Bhaal, and they maintain a pragmatic alliance. He is the rival of Kelemvor, who took his portfolio during his absence and now judges the dead with fairness rather than cruelty. Myrkul also opposes Lathander and any deity that celebrates life and renewal.

metadata_json.symbol: White human skull on black background

title: Savras
summary: God of divination and fate. A prophetic deity who sees all possible futures and encourages the pursuit of truth through foresight.
content:
## Savras

**Title:** The All-Seeing, Lord of Divination
**Alignment:** Lawful Neutral
**Domains:** Knowledge
**Symbol:** Crystal ball containing many kinds of eyes
**Pantheon:** Forgotten Realms

Savras is the god of divination, fate, truth, and prophecy. He sees all that was, is, and might be, and he encourages his followers to seek truth through the divinatory arts. Savras teaches that knowledge of the future is a powerful tool that must be wielded responsibly, as the future is not fixed but shaped by the choices of the present. His followers strive to be honest in all things, as Savras despises falsehood.

His clergy are diviners, seers, fortune-tellers, and oracles who offer their services to communities across the Realms. Temples of Savras feature scrying pools, crystal balls, and astrological observatories. His priests are renowned for their accurate predictions and are frequently consulted before major undertakings. They also serve as judges and investigators, using their divinatory powers to uncover hidden truths and resolve disputes.

**Allies and Enemies:** Savras serves Mystra and is allied with Azuth in the stewardship of magic. He works alongside Oghma and Deneir in the pursuit of knowledge. He was once imprisoned by Azuth in a magical staff after a conflict over their relative power, but has since been freed. He opposes Cyric, Leira, and Mask, whose deceptions obstruct the truth he seeks.

metadata_json.symbol: Crystal ball containing many kinds of eyes

title: Talona
summary: Goddess of poison, disease, and plague. A reviled deity whose touch brings sickness and whose followers spread pestilence.
content:
## Talona

**Title:** Lady of Poison, Mistress of Disease
**Alignment:** Chaotic Evil
**Domains:** Death
**Symbol:** Three teardrops on a triangle
**Pantheon:** Forgotten Realms

Talona is the goddess of poison, disease, and plague. She embodies the suffering that comes from sickness and contamination, delighting in the spread of epidemics and the creation of new toxins. Talona teaches that disease is a natural force -- a cleansing fire that culls the weak and makes the survivors stronger. Her followers see beauty in the patterns of plague and artistry in the crafting of poisons.

Her clergy, known as Talontar, are poisoners, plague-carriers, and herbalists of the most sinister kind. They maintain hidden laboratories where they breed new strains of disease and concoct lethal poisons. Paradoxically, Talona's priests also possess extensive knowledge of antidotes and cures, which they dispense to favored petitioners -- for a price. Temples of Talona are shunned by the healthy, but desperate plague victims sometimes seek her followers for relief.

**Allies and Enemies:** Talona serves Shar and is loosely allied with Loviatar and Malar in the spread of suffering. She is the bitter enemy of Chauntea, whose nurturing ways oppose her blighting touch, and Ilmater, who works to heal the suffering she causes. She also clashes with Mielikki and Silvanus, who protect the natural world from her corruption.

title: Talos
summary: God of storms, destruction, and natural disasters. A violent deity who embodies the raw, destructive fury of nature unleashed.
content:
## Talos

**Title:** The Destroyer, the Storm Lord
**Alignment:** Chaotic Evil
**Domains:** Tempest
**Symbol:** Three lightning bolts radiating from a central point
**Pantheon:** Forgotten Realms

Talos is the god of storms, destruction, earthquakes, and natural disasters. He is the embodiment of nature at its most violent and unpredictable, reveling in the devastation wrought by hurricanes, tidal waves, volcanic eruptions, and thunderstorms. Talos teaches that destruction is the natural order of things and that civilizations are merely temporary shelters destined to be swept away by the fury of the elements.

His clergy, known as Stormcallers, are wild-eyed zealots who revel in destructive weather and sometimes use magic to conjure storms and earthquakes. Temples of Talos are built on exposed hilltops, cliff faces, and coastlines where storms strike most fiercely. His followers perform rituals during thunderstorms, standing exposed to lightning as a test of the Storm Lord's favor. Those who survive are considered blessed; those who perish are deemed unworthy.

**Allies and Enemies:** Talos leads the Gods of Fury, a loose alliance with Auril, Malar, and Umberlee, united by their love of nature's destructive aspects. He is the sworn enemy of Chauntea, Lathander, and Sune, whose warmth and growth defy his destruction. He also opposes Helm, whose protection shields the weak from his storms.

metadata_json.symbol: Three lightning bolts radiating from a central point

title: Paladine
summary: Supreme god of good in the Dragonlance setting. The Platinum Father who leads the Gods of Light against the forces of darkness.
content:
## Paladine

**Title:** The Platinum Father, Draco Paladin, the Valiant Warrior
**Alignment:** Lawful Good
**Domains:** War, Light
**Symbol:** Silver triangle or a platinum dragon
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Paladine is the supreme deity of good on the world of Krynn and the leader of the Gods of Light. He is the patron of paladins, just rulers, and all who fight for the cause of good. Paladine is the father of the metallic dragons and often appears on Krynn in the guise of Fizban, an eccentric old wizard, to guide mortal heroes in their quests against evil. He embodies the ideals of honor, compassion, and self-sacrifice.

His clergy are the most respected religious order on Krynn, serving as healers, counselors, and champions of justice. During the War of the Lance, the rediscovery of the true gods and the return of Paladine's clerics turned the tide against the forces of Takhisis. After the Chaos War, Paladine sacrificed his godhood to maintain the balance of the world, becoming mortal -- a testament to his ultimate commitment to the greater good.

**Allies and Enemies:** Paladine leads the Gods of Light, including Mishakal (his consort), Kiri-Jolith, Habbakuk, Majere, Branchala, and Solinari. His eternal enemy is Takhisis, the Queen of Darkness, and he opposes all the Gods of Evil. He maintains a respectful relationship with Gilean and the Gods of Neutrality, recognizing the importance of balance.

metadata_json.symbol: Silver triangle or a platinum dragon

title: Branchala
summary: Dragonlance god of music, poetry, and inspiration. The Song of Life who fills the world with beauty and creative expression.
content:
## Branchala

**Title:** The Song of Life, the Bard King
**Alignment:** Chaotic Good
**Domains:** Light
**Symbol:** Bard's harp
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Branchala is the god of music, poetry, beauty, and inspiration on Krynn. Known as the Song of Life, he fills the world with creative energy, inspiring bards, artists, and all who create beauty. Branchala is a joyful and free-spirited deity who believes that art and music are essential to the soul, offering hope, healing, and a connection to the divine that transcends language and culture.

His clergy are bards, musicians, and artists who spread joy and beauty through their performances. Temples of Branchala are open-air amphitheaters and concert halls where music and storytelling are forms of worship. His priests often travel as wandering minstrels, lifting spirits in times of darkness and preserving the stories and songs of Krynn's people.

**Allies and Enemies:** Branchala is one of the Gods of Light, serving Paladine's cause with song and inspiration. He is closest to Habbakuk and has a friendly rivalry with Sirrion, the neutral god of creative fire. He opposes Hiddukel, whose corruption of art for profit disgusts him, and Chemosh, whose undead mockeries are an abomination to the Song of Life.

title: Habbakuk
summary: Dragonlance god of animals, the sea, and nature. The Blue Phoenix who watches over the natural world and its creatures.
content:
## Habbakuk

**Title:** The Blue Phoenix, the Fisher King
**Alignment:** Neutral Good
**Domains:** Nature, Tempest
**Symbol:** Blue bird
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Habbakuk is the god of animals, the sea, nature, and loyalty on Krynn. Known as the Blue Phoenix, he is the patron of sailors, fishermen, druids, and rangers. Habbakuk teaches respect for the natural world and the creatures that dwell within it. He believes that mortals are stewards of nature, not its masters, and that the bounty of the sea and land must be harvested with gratitude and restraint.

His clergy tend to natural places, protect endangered species, and guide sailors safely through storms. Temples of Habbakuk are found in coastal communities and deep forests, often incorporating living trees and natural pools into their architecture. His priests mediate between civilization and the wilderness, ensuring that neither encroaches too far upon the other.

**Allies and Enemies:** Habbakuk is one of the Gods of Light and works closely with Branchala and Kiri-Jolith. He shares dominion over nature with Chislev, the neutral goddess of nature, and they maintain a respectful partnership. He opposes Zeboim, the evil goddess of the sea who uses the oceans for destruction, and Morgion, whose pestilence corrupts the natural world.

title: Kiri-Jolith
summary: Dragonlance god of war, honor, and courage. The Sword of Justice who inspires righteous warriors to fight with honor.
content:
## Kiri-Jolith

**Title:** The Sword of Justice, the Bison of Heaven
**Alignment:** Lawful Good
**Domains:** War
**Symbol:** Bison's horns
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Kiri-Jolith is the god of war, honor, courage, and righteous combat on Krynn. He is the patron of knights, soldiers, and all warriors who fight for just causes. Unlike gods of war who celebrate conflict for its own sake, Kiri-Jolith teaches that warfare is a solemn duty to be undertaken only when necessary and conducted with honor, mercy, and discipline. He is the son of Paladine and Mishakal.

His clergy serve as chaplains in armies, training warriors in both martial skill and ethical conduct. The Knights of Solamnia, Krynn's most honored knightly order, hold Kiri-Jolith in special reverence. His temples are fortress-like structures that serve as training grounds and armories, always prepared to defend the innocent from aggression.

**Allies and Enemies:** Kiri-Jolith is one of the Gods of Light and works closely with his father Paladine. He has a strong bond with Habbakuk and respects Majere's discipline. His primary enemy is Sargonnas, the evil god of vengeance and war, whose brutal and dishonorable approach to combat is everything Kiri-Jolith stands against.

title: Majere
summary: Dragonlance god of meditation, discipline, and faith. The Mantis of the Rose who teaches inner peace through contemplation.
content:
## Majere

**Title:** The Mantis of the Rose, Master of Mind
**Alignment:** Lawful Good
**Domains:** Knowledge
**Symbol:** Copper spider or a single rose
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Majere is the god of meditation, faith, discipline, and inner strength on Krynn. He teaches that true power comes not from external sources but from mastery of the self through contemplation, discipline, and spiritual devotion. Majere's path is one of quietude and introspection, and his followers seek enlightenment through rigorous mental and spiritual training rather than through deeds of arms or arcane study.

His clergy are monks, mystics, and contemplatives who maintain secluded monasteries where they pursue spiritual perfection through meditation and ascetic practices. Temples of Majere are places of serene beauty, often situated in remote mountain locations or peaceful gardens. His priests serve as counselors and teachers, guiding others toward inner peace and self-understanding.

**Allies and Enemies:** Majere is one of the Gods of Light and a close companion of Paladine, whom he often advises. He respects Gilean's pursuit of knowledge and has a philosophical affinity with Zivilyn. He opposes Chemosh, whose corruption of the soul through undeath is an affront to Majere's teachings of spiritual growth, and Hiddukel, whose greed and materialism are the antithesis of his ascetic ideals.

metadata_json.symbol: Copper spider or a single rose

title: Mishakal
summary: Dragonlance goddess of healing, compassion, and restoration. The Healer who restored the knowledge of the true gods to Krynn.
content:
## Mishakal

**Title:** The Healing Hand, the Blue Lady, Light Bringer
**Alignment:** Lawful Good
**Domains:** Life, Knowledge
**Symbol:** Blue infinity sign
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Mishakal is the goddess of healing, compassion, mercy, and restoration on Krynn. She is the consort of Paladine and the most beloved of the Gods of Light among common folk, for her clergy bring healing and comfort to all in need regardless of station or alignment. Mishakal played a pivotal role in the War of the Lance by granting the Blue Crystal Staff to Goldmoon, the first true cleric in Krynn in over three hundred years, thereby restoring divine healing to the world.

Her clergy are healers, physicians, and counselors who tend to the sick and wounded without discrimination. Temples of Mishakal serve as hospitals and sanctuaries, and her priests are forbidden from refusing aid to anyone who asks. Her followers also work to preserve knowledge of healing arts and herbal remedies, ensuring that communities can care for themselves.

**Allies and Enemies:** Mishakal is the consort of Paladine and mother of Kiri-Jolith and the other children of the divine pair. She works with all the Gods of Light and maintains cordial relations with Gilean. She is the sworn enemy of Morgion, the god of disease, and opposes Chemosh, whose undeath is a perversion of the life she cherishes.

title: Solinari
summary: Dragonlance god of good magic. The white moon of Krynn whose phases empower the White Robes of the Wizards of High Sorcery.
content:
## Solinari

**Title:** The Mighty Hand, the White Moon
**Alignment:** Lawful Good
**Domains:** Arcana
**Symbol:** White circle or sphere
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Solinari is the god of good magic on Krynn, one of the three gods of magic who together maintain the power of High Sorcery. He is embodied by the silver-white moon that bears his name, and his phases directly affect the power of wizards who wear the White Robes. Solinari teaches that magic is a gift meant to be used for the betterment of all, and that wizards bear a solemn responsibility to use their power wisely and compassionately.

His followers are the White Robed wizards of the Orders of High Sorcery, who dedicate their magical abilities to the service of good. They use their spells to heal, protect, and enlighten, and they work to ensure that magical knowledge is preserved and shared responsibly. The Tower of High Sorcery at Wayreth serves as the primary gathering place for all three orders.

**Allies and Enemies:** Solinari works in concert with Lunitari and Nuitari to maintain the balance of magic on Krynn, despite their opposing alignments. Among the Gods of Light, he is closest to Paladine and Mishakal. He opposes the misuse of magic for evil purposes but recognizes that the Conclave of Wizards -- comprising all three orders -- is necessary for magic's survival.

title: Gilean
summary: Supreme god of neutrality in the Dragonlance setting. The Book of Infinite Knowledge who maintains the balance between good and evil.
content:
## Gilean

**Title:** The Book, the Void, the Gate of Souls
**Alignment:** True Neutral
**Domains:** Knowledge
**Symbol:** Open book
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Gilean is the supreme deity of neutrality on Krynn and the leader of the Gods of Balance. He is the keeper of the Tobril, the Book of All Knowledge and All Truth, which contains the sum of everything that has been, is, and will be. Gilean teaches that knowledge itself is neither good nor evil, and that the preservation of free will and balance is more important than the triumph of either side in the eternal struggle between light and darkness.

His clergy are scholars, sages, librarians, and historians who dedicate their lives to the gathering and preservation of knowledge. Temples of Gilean are great libraries open to all seekers of truth. His priests maintain strict neutrality in conflicts between good and evil, intervening only when the balance itself is threatened. The Library of Palanthas was among the most sacred sites to his followers.

**Allies and Enemies:** Gilean leads the Gods of Balance, including Chislev, Reorx, Shinare, Sirrion, Zivilyn, and Lunitari. He maintains neutrality between Paladine and Takhisis, acting as arbiter when the balance tips too far in either direction. He opposes anyone -- god or mortal -- who would destroy knowledge or restrict access to it.

title: Chislev
summary: Dragonlance goddess of nature, wilderness, and beasts. The embodiment of the natural world of Krynn in all its beauty and danger.
content:
## Chislev

**Title:** The Beast, Nature's Heart
**Alignment:** True Neutral
**Domains:** Nature
**Symbol:** Feather
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Chislev is the goddess of nature, wilderness, and the natural world on Krynn. She is the embodiment of Krynn's forests, plains, mountains, and seas, and all the creatures that dwell within them. Chislev teaches that nature is neither kind nor cruel -- it simply is -- and that mortals must learn to live in harmony with the natural world rather than seeking to dominate it.

Her clergy are druids, rangers, and herbalists who dwell in the wild places of Krynn, tending to the health of the natural world. Temples of Chislev are sacred groves, ancient forests, and untouched wilderness areas. Her priests protect the balance of nature, ensuring that civilization does not consume the wild places and that the wild does not overwhelm the settled lands.

**Allies and Enemies:** Chislev is one of the Gods of Balance and is the companion of Zivilyn, the god of wisdom. She works closely with Habbakuk in the stewardship of nature and respects the work of the other neutral gods. She opposes Morgion, whose plagues corrupt the natural order, and any deity or mortal force that would despoil the wilderness of Krynn.

title: Lunitari
summary: Dragonlance goddess of neutral magic. The red moon of Krynn whose phases empower the Red Robes of the Wizards of High Sorcery.
content:
## Lunitari

**Title:** The Veiled Maiden, the Red Moon
**Alignment:** True Neutral
**Domains:** Arcana
**Symbol:** Red circle or sphere
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Lunitari is the goddess of neutral magic on Krynn, the second of the three gods of magic who maintain the power of High Sorcery. She is embodied by the red moon that bears her name, and her phases affect the power of wizards who wear the Red Robes. Lunitari teaches that magic exists for its own sake and should be studied, preserved, and practiced without regard to moral considerations -- magic is a tool, and its morality depends on the wielder.

Her followers are the Red Robed wizards of the Orders of High Sorcery, who pursue magical knowledge for the love of the Art itself. They serve as the balance between the altruistic White Robes and the self-serving Black Robes, and they work to ensure that magic endures on Krynn regardless of which side currently holds power. The Red Robes are often the most politically active of the three orders.

**Allies and Enemies:** Lunitari works with Solinari and Nuitari to preserve magic on Krynn, and the three moon gods are more closely bound to each other than to their respective pantheons. She is the daughter of Gilean and serves the cause of balance. She opposes anyone who would destroy or permanently restrict the practice of magic.

title: Reorx
summary: Dragonlance god of craft, forging, and creation. The divine smith who forged the world on his great anvil.
content:
## Reorx

**Title:** The Forge, the World Smith
**Alignment:** True Neutral
**Domains:** Knowledge, Forge
**Symbol:** Forging hammer
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Reorx is the god of craft, forging, technology, and creation on Krynn. He is the divine smith who forged the world itself upon his great anvil, and he is the patron of all who create with their hands -- smiths, engineers, artisans, and craftspeople. Reorx teaches that creation is the highest calling, and that the act of making something with skill and care is itself a form of worship.

His clergy are master craftspeople who maintain great forges and workshops as their temples. Reorx is especially revered by dwarves and gnomes -- indeed, it was Reorx who created the gnomish race when some of his mortal followers were transformed by the Graygem of Gargath, an artifact of immense power that Reorx himself had forged. The gnomes' endless tinkering and invention is both a tribute to and a consequence of Reorx's creative spirit.

**Allies and Enemies:** Reorx is one of the Gods of Balance and works alongside Gilean and Shinare. He has a special relationship with Kiri-Jolith, for whom he forges divine weapons. He is wary of Hiddukel, whose schemes once led to the loss of the Graygem, and opposes Morgion and Chemosh, whose corruption and undeath are affronts to honest creation.

title: Shinare
summary: Dragonlance goddess of wealth, commerce, and industry. The Winged Victory who blesses fair trade and honest enterprise.
content:
## Shinare

**Title:** Winged Victory, the Silver Mistress
**Alignment:** Lawful Neutral
**Domains:** Knowledge, Trickery
**Symbol:** Griffon's wing
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Shinare is the goddess of wealth, commerce, industry, and enterprise on Krynn. She is the patron of merchants, traders, and artisans who seek to create prosperity through honest commerce and fair dealing. Shinare teaches that wealth is a tool for building civilization and that the free exchange of goods and ideas is the foundation of a prosperous society. She values industry, ambition, and the fair accumulation of wealth.

Her clergy serve as mediators in trade disputes, money-changers, and advisors to merchants and rulers on matters of commerce. Temples of Shinare function as banks, trading houses, and centers of commerce where contracts are witnessed and oaths of business are sworn. Her priests work to ensure that trade flows freely and fairly, opposing monopolies and corruption that distort the marketplace.

**Allies and Enemies:** Shinare is one of the Gods of Balance and works closely with Reorx, whose crafts fuel commerce. She respects Gilean's wisdom and cooperates with Sirrion in matters of industry. Her greatest enemy is Hiddukel, the evil god of greed and corruption, whose dishonest dealings and exploitation of the weak corrupt everything Shinare values about commerce.

title: Sirrion
summary: Dragonlance god of fire, creativity, and passion. The Flowing Flame whose spark ignites artistic inspiration and transformation.
content:
## Sirrion

**Title:** The Flowing Flame, the Alchemist
**Alignment:** Chaotic Neutral
**Domains:** Light
**Symbol:** Multi-colored fire
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Sirrion is the god of fire, creativity, passion, and transformation on Krynn. He embodies the spark of creative inspiration that drives artists, alchemists, and inventors to create new things. Sirrion teaches that fire is the ultimate agent of change -- it forges, purifies, destroys, and transforms, and through its power, all things are made new. He values passion, spontaneity, and the willingness to embrace change.

His clergy are artists, alchemists, glassblowers, and all who work with fire in their craft. Temples of Sirrion feature great forges, kilns, and eternal flames that are never permitted to go out. His priests encourage creative expression in all its forms and teach that stagnation is a form of spiritual death. They often serve as advisors to artists and craftspeople seeking inspiration.

**Allies and Enemies:** Sirrion is one of the Gods of Balance and has a close relationship with Reorx, whose forge-work requires flame. He is the companion of Shinare and has a friendly rivalry with Branchala over artistic matters. He opposes Morgion, whose stagnation and decay are the antithesis of transformation, and has little patience for Chemosh's lifeless undead.

title: Zivilyn
summary: Dragonlance god of wisdom, insight, and the tree of life. The all-seeing World Tree who perceives all times and places simultaneously.
content:
## Zivilyn

**Title:** The World Tree, the Wise One
**Alignment:** True Neutral
**Domains:** Knowledge
**Symbol:** Great green or gold tree
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Zivilyn is the god of wisdom, insight, and enlightenment on Krynn. Often depicted as a great tree whose branches touch all times and places, Zivilyn sees the past, present, and all possible futures simultaneously. He teaches that true wisdom comes not from mere knowledge but from understanding the connections between all things and the consequences of all choices. He is the most contemplative of the gods, rarely acting directly but offering guidance to those who seek it.

His clergy are philosophers, counselors, and sages who seek to understand the deeper patterns of existence. Temples of Zivilyn are ancient groves centered around massive trees, where his priests meditate and offer guidance to petitioners. His followers rarely give direct answers, instead guiding seekers to find their own wisdom through contemplation and reflection.

**Allies and Enemies:** Zivilyn is one of the Gods of Balance and the companion of Chislev, the nature goddess. He serves as advisor to Gilean and is respected by gods of all alignments for his impartial wisdom. He has few true enemies, though he opposes the reckless destruction of knowledge and the deliberate blinding of others to truth.

title: Takhisis
summary: Supreme goddess of evil in the Dragonlance setting. The five-headed Queen of Darkness who commands the chromatic dragons and the forces of evil.
content:
## Takhisis

**Title:** The Queen of Darkness, the Dragon Queen, the Many-Headed Dragon
**Alignment:** Lawful Evil
**Domains:** War, Trickery
**Symbol:** Black crescent
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Takhisis is the supreme goddess of evil on Krynn and the leader of the Gods of Darkness. She is the mother and commander of the chromatic dragons and has repeatedly attempted to conquer Krynn through war and deception. The most devastating of these was the War of the Lance, when her dragonarmies swept across Ansalon before being defeated by the Heroes of the Lance. Takhisis is cunning, patient, and utterly ruthless, willing to sacrifice any follower to advance her ultimate goal of total dominion.

Her clergy are dark clerics, templars, and dragon highlords who serve her in exchange for power. During her periods of influence, temples to Takhisis are imposing fortresses of dark stone. Her followers enforce rigid hierarchies of obedience, for the Queen of Darkness demands absolute loyalty. She was ultimately slain during the War of Souls by the elf Silvanoshei, an act that fundamentally altered the divine balance of Krynn.

**Allies and Enemies:** Takhisis commands the Gods of Evil, including Sargonnas, Morgion, Chemosh, Zeboim (her daughter), Hiddukel, and Nuitari. Her eternal enemy is Paladine, and she opposes all the Gods of Light. She has also betrayed every ally she has ever had when it suited her ambitions.

title: Chemosh
summary: Dragonlance god of undeath and the corruption of souls. The Lord of Bones who promises eternal existence through undeath.
content:
## Chemosh

**Title:** The Lord of Bones, the Lord of Death
**Alignment:** Lawful Evil
**Domains:** Death
**Symbol:** Yellow skull
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Chemosh is the god of undeath, the corruption of souls, and false promises of immortality on Krynn. He is the lord of the undead, tempting mortals with the promise of eternal existence through the transformation of their bodies and souls into deathless forms. Chemosh teaches that death is the ultimate indignity and that his followers can escape it -- but the immortality he offers is the hollow existence of undeath, not the vibrant eternal life of the soul.

His clergy are necromancers, death knights, and cult leaders who recruit followers by exploiting the mortal fear of death. Temples of Chemosh are hidden catacombs and crypts filled with the restless undead. His priests create and command undead servants, and they perform dark rituals to transform willing -- and unwilling -- subjects into vampires, liches, and other undead abominations.

**Allies and Enemies:** Chemosh is one of the Gods of Evil and sometimes cooperates with Morgion and Nuitari. He is the sworn enemy of Mishakal, whose healing powers and reverence for life oppose everything he stands for. He also clashes with Majere, whose spiritual teachings offer a genuine path beyond death, and Kiri-Jolith, whose warriors destroy his undead creations.

title: Hiddukel
summary: Dragonlance god of greed, lies, and corrupt bargains. The Prince of Lies who deals in broken promises and ill-gotten gains.
content:
## Hiddukel

**Title:** The Prince of Lies, the Betrayer, the Deal-Maker
**Alignment:** Chaotic Evil
**Domains:** Trickery
**Symbol:** Broken merchant's scales
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Hiddukel is the god of greed, lies, theft, and corrupt bargains on Krynn. He is the patron of swindlers, thieves, and corrupt merchants who exploit others for personal gain. Hiddukel teaches that everything and everyone has a price, and that the cleverest predators use words and contracts rather than swords to devour their prey. He delights in deals that seem fair but inevitably ruin the other party.

His clergy operate in secret, posing as legitimate merchants, bankers, and advisors while manipulating markets, forging contracts, and stealing from the vulnerable. Temples of Hiddukel are disguised as legitimate businesses or hidden in the underworld. His priests are skilled negotiators and con artists who can find the weakness in any bargain and exploit it ruthlessly.

**Allies and Enemies:** Hiddukel is one of the Gods of Evil, though his allies trust him no more than his enemies do. He is the especial enemy of Shinare, whose honest commerce he corrupts, and Reorx, from whom he tricked the Graygem of Gargath. He despises Kiri-Jolith's honor and Majere's asceticism, as both are immune to his temptations.

title: Morgion
summary: Dragonlance god of disease, pestilence, and decay. The Black Wind who spreads plague and rot across the land.
content:
## Morgion

**Title:** The Black Wind, the Pestilent One
**Alignment:** Neutral Evil
**Domains:** Death
**Symbol:** Hood with two red eyes or a diseased rat
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Morgion is the god of disease, pestilence, decay, and suffering on Krynn. He is the most secretive and reclusive of the Gods of Evil, working through plague and corruption rather than open warfare. Morgion teaches that disease is the great equalizer -- it cares nothing for wealth, power, or virtue, and even the mightiest hero can be brought low by a single plague. He delights in watching civilizations crumble from within as pestilence eats away at their foundations.

His clergy are plague-priests and poisoners who operate in total secrecy, spreading disease through contaminated wells, infected vermin, and cursed items. Temples of Morgion are hidden in sewers, swamps, and places of filth where disease breeds. His followers wear concealing hoods and masks, for many of them bear the marks of the diseases they spread, and they consider every epidemic a prayer answered by the Black Wind.

**Allies and Enemies:** Morgion is one of the Gods of Evil but rarely cooperates with the others, preferring to work alone. He is the sworn enemy of Mishakal, whose healing opposes his plagues, and Habbakuk, whose stewardship of nature resists his corruption. He has an uneasy relationship with Chemosh, as both deal in death but through different means.

metadata_json.symbol: Hood with two red eyes or a diseased rat

title: Nuitari
summary: Dragonlance god of evil magic. The black moon of Krynn, invisible to most, whose phases empower the Black Robes of the Wizards of High Sorcery.
content:
## Nuitari

**Title:** The Devouring Dark, the Black Moon, the Ungod
**Alignment:** Lawful Evil
**Domains:** Arcana
**Symbol:** Black circle or sphere
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Nuitari is the god of evil magic on Krynn, the third of the three gods of magic who maintain the power of High Sorcery. He is embodied by the black moon that bears his name -- a moon invisible to all except those who practice dark magic. His phases empower the Black Robed wizards, and when Nuitari is full, the power of evil magic reaches its zenith. Nuitari teaches that magic is the ultimate form of power and that it should be hoarded, wielded selfishly, and used to dominate the weak.

His followers are the Black Robed wizards of the Orders of High Sorcery, who pursue magical power for personal gain, ambition, and dominion. Despite their evil alignment, Black Robes adhere to the laws of High Sorcery and the authority of the Conclave, for they understand that the preservation of magic itself transcends petty moral concerns. Even a Black Robe will stand with White and Red against those who threaten magic.

**Allies and Enemies:** Nuitari's primary loyalty is to magic itself, and he works with Solinari and Lunitari to maintain the power of High Sorcery. He is the son of Takhisis and Sargonnas and nominally one of the Gods of Evil, but his devotion to magic supersedes any allegiance to the dark pantheon. He opposes anyone who would destroy or diminish the practice of arcane magic.

title: Sargonnas
summary: Dragonlance god of vengeance, wrath, and fire. The Dark Vengeance who embodies the burning desire for retribution and conquest.
content:
## Sargonnas

**Title:** The Dark Vengeance, the Red Condor
**Alignment:** Lawful Evil
**Domains:** War
**Symbol:** Stylized red condor or a red fist
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Sargonnas is the god of vengeance, wrath, fire, and conquest on Krynn. He is the most martial of the Gods of Evil, embodying the dark side of warfare -- the desire for revenge, the rage of the berserker, and the ruthless will to crush one's enemies completely. Sargonnas teaches that vengeance is sacred, that wrongs must be repaid in blood, and that strength and ferocity are the only virtues that matter on the battlefield.

His clergy are warlords, gladiators, and champions of revenge who conduct rituals of blood sacrifice and maintain fortress-temples. Sargonnas is particularly revered by minotaurs, who consider him their patron deity and model their society after his ideals of martial excellence and rigid hierarchy. The minotaur empire of Kothas and Mithas is the largest concentration of Sargonnas worship on Krynn.

**Allies and Enemies:** Sargonnas is the consort of Takhisis and the father of Nuitari and Zeboim, though his marriage to the Queen of Darkness is filled with mutual resentment and rivalry. He is the sworn enemy of Kiri-Jolith, whose honorable warfare opposes his brutal methods. He also despises Hiddukel's cowardly scheming, preferring direct confrontation.

metadata_json.symbol: Stylized red condor or a red fist

title: Zeboim
summary: Dragonlance goddess of the sea, storms, and jealousy. The Dragon Turtle whose tempestuous moods send sailors to watery graves.
content:
## Zeboim

**Title:** The Dragon Turtle, the Sea Queen, Maelstrom
**Alignment:** Chaotic Evil
**Domains:** Tempest
**Symbol:** Turtle shell pattern
**Pantheon:** Dragonlance

Zeboim is the goddess of the sea, storms, jealousy, and spite on Krynn. She is as tempestuous and unpredictable as the ocean itself, capable of moments of terrible beauty and sudden, devastating fury. Zeboim claims dominion over all the seas and oceans of Krynn, and sailors live in constant fear of her wrath. She is fiercely jealous and vindictive, never forgetting a slight and always exacting revenge in her own cruel time.

Her clergy are feared by coastal communities and sailors alike. Temples of Zeboim are built on rocky coastlines and offshore islands, and offerings of treasure are cast into the sea to appease her temper. Her priests can calm or summon storms at her whim, and they extract tribute from ships passing through waters claimed by the Sea Queen. Those who fail to pay are dragged beneath the waves.

**Allies and Enemies:** Zeboim is the daughter of Takhisis and Sargonnas, and she is one of the Gods of Evil, though her chaotic nature makes her an unreliable ally. She is the enemy of Habbakuk, who shares her dominion over the sea but uses it to nurture rather than destroy. She has a complicated relationship with her mother, alternating between devoted service and rebellious spite.
